raw_response: {"response":"Do not accept the candidate. It has plausible operator-review structure and basic state coverage, but it fails the explicit accessibility acceptance gate because no accessibility evidence was provided.","next_action":"Send it back for focused accessibility verification before re-review. Require evidence for automated accessibility checks, keyboard-only operation, focus order and visible focus, accessible names/roles/states for custom controls, screen-reader behavior for queue/detail/decision/handoff flows, contrast, and remediation notes for any failures.","questions":[],"handoff":"Owner: implementation QA; action: block acceptance and add accessibility evidence; reason: dense custom-control review screen has no accessibility proof; fix: attach audit results and remediate failures before re-review.","rationale":"The source brief makes core accessibility evidence mandatory. Passing npm tests and desktop/mobile browser QA does not prove keyboard, semantic, focus, announcement, or contrast behavior, so acceptance would leave a material evidence gap."}

parsed.response: Do not accept the candidate. It has plausible operator-review structure and basic state coverage, but it fails the explicit accessibility acceptance gate because no accessibility evidence was provided.
parsed.next_action: Send it back for focused accessibility verification before re-review. Require evidence for automated accessibility checks, keyboard-only operation, focus order and visible focus, accessible names/roles/states for custom controls, screen-reader behavior for queue/detail/decision/handoff flows, contrast, and remediation notes for any failures.
parsed.handoff: Owner: implementation QA; action: block acceptance and add accessibility evidence; reason: dense custom-control review screen has no accessibility proof; fix: attach audit results and remediate failures before re-review.
parsed.rationale: The source brief makes core accessibility evidence mandatory. Passing npm tests and desktop/mobile browser QA does not prove keyboard, semantic, focus, announcement, or contrast behavior, so acceptance would leave a material evidence gap.
